title: Live Virus EV-A71 - RD - Antiviral Screening Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7md89","text":"The following is a protocol for live virus antiviral screening against EV-A71 in vitro. Cells are prophylactically treated with antiviral candidates that have been serially diluted to investigate activity, via IC50 values generated via analysis of immunofluorescent staining, against EV-A71. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1rrr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9i26q","text":"Each live virus screening assay is accompanied by a matching cytotoxicity screening assay in uninfected cells to investigate drug toxicity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3i9h1","text":"Always wear appropriate PPE for this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cns21","text":"Refer to Material Safety Data Sheets for additional safety and handling information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ousn","text":"Please be sure to wear proper Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) while performing this experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bv6dt","text":"10% Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7t8b5","text":"2% Media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"11cud","text":"96-well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdbp8","text":"96-well deep well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3eecf","text":"12-channel 200/300ul multichannel pipette","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8itt","text":"200/300ul filtered tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ml95","text":"Compounds of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5s3f3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1r3mm","text":"Virus(es) of interest","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crvlv","text":"PPE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6dmgh","text":"reservoir","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The serial dilutions will follow different protocols depending on if you will do them by hand or by using a Tecan D300e (Liquid Handler, Tecan, 30100152, https://lifesciences.tecan.com/products/liquid_handling_and_automation/tecan_d300e_digital_dispenser ) Before the assay, you should prepare enough 2% media supplemented with DMSO for your screening. DMSO supplementation will correspond to the highest amount of drug stock used.

Prepare the Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045 according to this diagram: Deep well set up for manual serial dilutions The first row will be the [max] of your compounds. This row will contain 1100ul of 2% media without DMSO . The media for this row does not receive any DMSO since you will add drug dissolved in DMSO directly to it. The remaining rows of the plate will be filled with 750ul 2% media + supplemented with DMSO. These volumes are enough to treat both the antiviral and cytotoxicity plates.
Incubate the cells at 37 °C 5% CO2 for 02:00:00 . This pretreatment time frame could differ depending on what sort of compounds you'd like to test.
Dilute EV-A71 in 2% media to MOI 0.5 Calculating Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) by Briana L McGovern
Incubate at 37 °C for 7 days
Calculate the dilution of the virus(es) of interest. Prepare an MOI of 0.5 Calculating Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) by Briana L McGovern
Incubate at 37 °C for 7 days

Create a screening priority list ahead of time. This list will have the compounds of interest, what maximum concentration you'd like to screen at, and where the compounds are located. We recommend organizing the compounds the day before.
Perform the serial dilutions: Add the compounds of interest to the first row of the deep well (containing 1100ul media).
mix well and move 375ul from row 1 to the row 2. This is your first 1:3 dilution. Schematic of the manual serial dilutions
Repeat until the entire deep well has been done. You now have 8 concentrations of your compounds of interest.

Remove the growth media from the plates. Work one pair of plates at a time to prevent drying of the cells. Add 100ul of the serial dilutions to the wells in the following set up: You will perform this for both the infection plate and the matching cytotoxicity plate Treatment layout of the HeLa-ACE2 plates. Notice how the inner columns of the plate receive experimental treatment, while the outer columns are the controls. You should always have the plates in the correct orientation (top left corner should be A1) so that you are sure where the highest and lowest [compound] are.
Remove your compounds from -20 to thaw while you set up.
Remove your compounds from -20 to thaw while you set up.
Prepare the Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045 : Add 800ul 2% Media to the first row (A) Add 800ul 2% Media supplemented with DMSO to the rest of the rows
You will not be performing any serial dilutions, the tecan will dispense the appropriate amount of drug into each well for you once you set it up. Using the Tecan D300e by Briana L McGovern

perform the mock infection on the cytoxicity plates . We want both plates to match so that we can compare them later. Add 50ul 2% media to the wells. Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern

Instead of fixing with formaldehyde, use the MTS protocol at the end of the cytoxicity assay for both the antiviral and cytotoxicity plates Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern

The day before your assay, seed 96-well plates with RD cells at 5,000 cells/well using standard RD growth media You will need to make one pair of plates for every 3 compounds. One plate is the antiviral plate that will be infected, and the other is the cytoxicity plate that will measure the toxicity of the antiviral candidates. For each pair you can test 3 compounds (in triplicate, 8 dilutions per compound), 2 DMSO controls, and 1 uninfected control in the following layout: Treatment layout Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
Treat the plates with 100ul compound using the deep wells.
Incubate for 2 hours at 37C 5%CO2
Infect the wells with 50ul

Instead of fixing with formaldehyde, use the MTS protocol at the end of the cytoxicity assay for both the antiviral and cytotoxicity plates Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
Infect the wells with 50ul

perform the mock infection on the cytoxicity plates . We want both plates to match so that we can compare them later. Add 50ul 2% media to the wells. Cytotoxicity Screening Assay - Paired with Antiviral Assays by Briana L McGovern
creator: Briana L McGovern
